tool_args.label: LIRR & Metro-North Key Performance Metrics - January 2024

payload.title: LONG ISLAND RAIL ROAD & METRO-NORTH RAILROAD KEY PERFORMANCE METRICS
payload.description: Key performance metrics document prepared for the January 2024 meeting of the Joint Long Island Rail Road & Metro-North Railroad Committees.

warnings: source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.

payload.description: LIRR is comprised of over 7,000 employees serving over 200,000 passengers a day. We operate 700 trains daily and maintain 125 stations, nearly 700 miles of track, and 27 shops and yards.

Metro-North Railroad is comprised of nearly 6,000 employees serving over 200,000 passengers a day. We operate 700 trains daily and maintain 124 stations, nearly 900 miles of track, and 19 shops and yards.

The Metropolitan Transportation Authority is North America's largest transportation network, serving a population of 15.3 million people across a 5,000 square-mile travel area surrounding New York City through Long Island, southeastern New York State, and Connecticut.
MTA Construction & Development partnered with LIRR and Metro-North on major projects including signal modernization and station roof rehabilitation.

payload.title: Acting President, Long Island Rail Road

payload.description: MTA Police Department enforces safety and security on LIRR and Metro-North.
NJ Transit operates West of Hudson trains for Metro-North Railroad.

Grand Central Madison terminal opened January 2023, introduced new commuting options on the LIRR. Since opening, the terminal has been the origin or destination of 17.1 million trips and currently 289 trains operate daily during the week.

LIRR and MTA Construction & Development are replacing signal equipment beyond its useful life at Babylon Interlocking on the Babylon and Montauk branches with a new, modern signal system. The existing system was installed in the 1960s. Uses design-build contracting.

tool_args.label: Tuckahoe and Hastings Stations Roof Rehabilitation

payload.project_name: Tuckahoe and Hastings Stations Roof Rehabilitation
payload.description: Metro-North and MTA Construction & Development completed roof rehabilitations at Tuckahoe Station (Harlem Line) and Hastings Station (Hudson Line). Both feature Spanish terracotta roofs. Project fully replaced tiles, repaired water damage, and replaced materials in-kind per historic registry requirements.

tool_args.label: Scarsdale and Hartsdale Stations Accessibility Improvements

payload.project_name: Scarsdale and Hartsdale Stations Accessibility Improvements
payload.description: Both Scarsdale and Hartsdale Stations on the Harlem Line made fully accessible. Scarsdale received a second elevator. Hartsdale got two new lifts. Includes new ADA-compliant sidewalks, walkways, and overpass enhancements.

tool_args.label: Park Avenue Tunnel Double Slip Switch Replacement

payload.project_name: Park Avenue Tunnel Double Slip Switch and Diamond Switch Replacement
payload.description: Metro-North began a replacement program on January 12 for three critical double slip switches and a diamond switch that allows trains to operate from any track in the Park Avenue Tunnel to any platform within GCT. These switches have been in service since 2000. Work through late March. New train schedules include cancellations and combinations with added cars.

In 2024, LIRR will continue enhancing station accessibility and Laurelton Station is next to begin platform reconstruction. Over three weekends in February, service will be adjusted to accommodate construction.
Purdy's Station, already accessible with two elevators, is scheduled to complete its accessibility improvements later this year.

MTA is moving forward with ADA upgrades at three Bronx Metro-North stations: Woodlawn, Williams Bridge (new platforms, elevators, amenities), and Botanical Garden Station (rehabilitation and elevator upgrades).

MTA officials celebrated the one-year anniversary of the historic Grand Central Madison opening, which introduced new commuting options on the LIRR. GCM saw its very first customers with the January 25, 2023 introduction of a month-long shuttle service.

tool_args.label: Grand Central Madison Full Service Introduction

payload.description: Introduction of full service at Grand Central Madison, following the month-long shuttle service that began January 25, 2023.
LIRR's Government & Community Relations team hosted their first steMTA program at Babylon Memorial Grade School in Suffolk County, focused on climate change and STEM principles.

tool_args.label: LIRR First New Year's Eve with Two Terminals

payload.description: LIRR successfully ran its first New Year's Eve program operating out of two terminals (Penn Station and Grand Central Madison).
A new one-day weekend ridership record of 149,000 set on Saturday, December 15, 2023 for Metro-North.

tool_args.label: Penn Station Central Control Supervisory System Loss

payload.description: A loss of supervisory system at Penn Station Central Control caused 58 late trains, delayed customers an average of 24 minutes and reduced monthly OTP by less than one percent.

payload.raw_value_text: 41% increase in service that came along with it
payload.context: Service increase driven by Grand Central Madison opening

tool_args.label: LIRR Additional Trains Since GCM Full Service

payload.raw_value_text: 77,000 additional LIRR trains operated since debut of GCM full schedules
payload.context: Trains operated in 10 months since GCM full service

payload.raw_value_text: 56% increase in reverse-peak LIRR service beginning February 2023
payload.description: 56% increase in reverse peak service, amounting to approximately 151 daily trains

tool_args.label: LIRR Grand Central Madison December 2023 Ridership
Metro-North 2023 Total Ridership East and West of Hudson

payload.raw_value_text: a new one-day record of 149,000 set on Saturday, December 15

tool_args.label: Metro-North Mean Distance Between Failures November 2023
LIRR Mean Distance Between Failures November 2023
LIRR Grand Central Madison First Year Ridership

payload.raw_value_text: 10.6 million customers traveled to or from Grand Central Madison in the past year

payload.claim_text: In December, on-time performance (OTP) was 94.6%, above the goal of 94%.
payload.subject: Long Island Rail Road

tool_args.label: Metro-North 4th Consecutive Year Above OTP Goal

payload.claim_text: 2023 OTP was above 97%, marking the fourth consecutive year Metro-North exceeded its OTP goal. This is only the second time in the history of the railroad that we have accomplished this level of performance.
payload.subject: Metro-North Railroad
